---
title: "Orientation"
subtitle: "What the KG is, what it contains, and how to talk to it"
---

## Overview {#sec-orientation}

This chapter is your starting point. Before running any query, spend a few minutes here to understand how the KG is structured—you will get significantly better results.

---

## What is the Knowledge Graph? {#sec-kg-intro}

A **Knowledge Graph (KG)** is a structured, semantic representation of data that connects real-world entities—genes, proteins, metabolites, pathways, samples, publications—through typed relationships.

The BMS CHO Productivity KG uses a **property graph** model (Neo4j) with three primitives:

| Primitive | Description | Examples |
|---|---|---|
| **Nodes** (Entities) | Objects or concepts | Gene, Protein, Metabolite, Sample, Pathway, Factor, Cohort, Study |
| **Edges** (Connections) | Typed relationships | `gene_expressed_in`, `upregulated`, `gene_part_of`, `tf_regulates_cohort_degs` |
| **Properties** (Attributes) | Metadata on nodes/edges | log2FC, adj. p-value, kME, MOFA weight, Spearman r |

The KG is queried through a natural-language interface that converts your question into Cypher graph queries automatically. You can also use the NLQ tips in @sec-query-tips.

---

## Data Model Overview

The complete data model connects the following entity types:

```{=html}
<figure>
<figcaption><strong>Figure 1.1</strong> — Simplified entity-relationship diagram of the BMS KG. Arrows represent named edge types; colors group entity categories (blue = molecular features, pink = experimental design, orange/tan = phenotypes, purple = pathway annotations).</figcaption>
</figure>
```

Key relationships to know:

- **Gene → Sample**: `gene_expressed_in` (TPM values, per sample)
- **Gene → Cohort**: `upregulated` / `downregulated` / `non_regulated` (DESeq2 results)
- **Gene / Protein / Metabolite → Network**: `gene_part_of` (WGCNA module membership, kME)
- **Gene / Protein / Metabolite → Factor**: `gene_contributes_to_factor` (MOFA weights)
- **Gene → Positive/Negative Phenotypes**: Spearman correlation with qP, titre, IVCD
- **Gene → Cohort**: `tf_regulates_cohort_degs` (TF enrichment)
- **Pathway → Cohort / Network / Factor**: `kegg_pathway_enriched_in` / `go_term_enriched_in`

---

## What Data Lives in the KG? {#sec-data-contents}

### In-House BMS Data

**Study 1** — multi-omics time-series (Days 3, 5, 9, 11, 14):

| Molecule | Clones | High qP | Low qP | Omics |
|---|---|---|---|---|
| C144 | 5 | 2 | 3 | Transcriptomics, Proteomics, Metabolomics |
| Nivo | 3 | 1 | 2 | Transcriptomics, Proteomics, Metabolomics |

**Study 2** — proteomics only (Days 5, 7, 9, 11):

| Molecule | Clones | High qP | Low qP |
|---|---|---|---|
| Milvaxin | 6 | 3 | 3 |
| COVID2 | 6 | 3 | 3 |
| Nivo | 3 | 1 | 2 |
| FXIaR | 6 | 3 | 3 |

Bioprocess measurements in Study 1: **qP, titre, IVCD, lactate, O₂, cell density**, and others.

### Public Data

- **11 transcriptomics datasets** from 10 publications (Study 1 + public), comparing high vs. low or high vs. non-producing clones
- **3 proteomics datasets** (Studies 1 & 2 + public)
- **1 metabolomics dataset** (Study 1)
- **~500 publications** on genetic perturbation → productivity outcomes (45 ingested at the time of writing)

### External Databases

NCBI Gene, UniProt, ChEBI, KEGG (pathways, reactions, enzymes, orthologs), Gene Ontology (BP, MF, CC), PubMed, Cellosaurus.

---

## Cohort Naming Conventions {#sec-cohorts}

The KG stores results for **883 cohorts** across four categories. When querying for DEG or enrichment results, **always name your cohort explicitly** for best retrieval.

| Category | Count | Example |
|---|---|---|
| Productivity-Based (High vs. Low, all clones & days) | 6 | `Nivo_study1_all_high_vs_Nivo_study1_all_low` |
| Clone-Wise (across all days) | 36 | `Nivo_clone8_study1_all_vs_Nivo_clone49_study1_all` |
| Temporal (same clone, different days) | 190 | `Nivo_clone49_day11_study1_vs_Nivo_clone49_day8_study1` |
| Temporal Cross-Clone | 630 | `Nivo_clone8_day11_study1_vs_Nivo_clone49_day8_study1` |
| Public (high vs. low / non-producer) | 19 | `GSE295486_high_producer_vs_GSE295486_non_producer` |

::: {.callout-tip}
Prefer **Productivity-Based** cohorts when asking global "which genes drive high productivity?" questions. Use Clone-Wise or Temporal cohorts for clone-specific or time-resolved investigations.
:::

---

## How to Phrase Natural-Language Queries {#sec-query-tips}

The KG interface accepts plain English. Follow these principles for reliable results:

1. **Name the entity type** — say "gene", "protein", or "metabolite", not just a symbol.
2. **Name the cohort or condition** — include molecule (Nivo / C144) and study when relevant.
3. **State the direction** — "upregulated in high producers", "negatively correlated with qP".
4. **State the evidence layer** — "in WGCNA module M1", "with MOFA Factor 1 weight > 0.3".
5. **Add thresholds explicitly** — "adj. p-value < 0.05 and |log2FC| > 2".

::: {.query-box}
Show me genes upregulated in high vs. low producers in Nivo Study 1 with adj. p-value < 0.05 and absolute log2 fold change greater than 2.
:::

::: {.query-box}
Which proteins are positively correlated with qP (Spearman r > 0.5) in Study 1?
:::

::: {.query-box}
What KEGG pathways are enriched in WGCNA Module M1 for the Nivo high vs. low productivity cohort?
:::

---

## Understanding Query Results

Results are returned as **tables**, **network views**, or **charts** depending on the query type:

- **Tables** — for DEG lists, correlation rankings, module membership
- **Network views** — for TF-gene regulatory graphs, WGCNA modules, KG subgraphs
- **Bar / violin charts** — for pathway enrichment scores, correlation distributions

Key columns you will encounter:

| Column | Meaning |
|---|---|
| `log2FC` | Log₂ fold change (DEG); positive = higher in first group |
| `adj_pvalue` | Benjamini-Hochberg corrected p-value |
| `kME` | Module eigengene connectivity (WGCNA hub score; closer to 1 = stronger hub) |
| `gs_qp` / `gs_titre` / `gs_ivcd` | Gene significance scores (WGCNA correlation with trait) |
| `mofa_weight` | MOFA feature weight; sign indicates direction, magnitude indicates importance |
| `spearman_r` | Spearman correlation of feature abundance with productivity phenotype |
| `NES` | Normalised enrichment score (GSEA); positive = enriched in first group |
